title: Math — Rates, Ratios, and Units
description: SAT-style problems involving unit conversions, ratios, proportional reasoning, rates, and density.

questions.prompt: The International Space Station orbits Earth at an average speed of \(4.76\) miles per second. What is the station’s average speed in miles per hour?
questions.explanation: There are 3,600 seconds in an hour. Multiply: \(4.76 \times 3600 = 17{,}136\) miles per hour.

questions.prompt: Based on the table, what is the ratio of the cost per gram of protein in a large egg to the cost per gram of protein in a cup of milk?
How many tablespoons are equivalent to 14 teaspoons? (3 teaspoons = 1 tablespoon)
A distance of 112 furlongs is equivalent to how many feet? (1 furlong = 220 yards and 1 yard = 3 feet)
Paul Revere rode 11 miles in 26 minutes. What was his average speed, to the nearest tenth of a mile per hour?
questions.explanation: Convert minutes to hours: \(26/60\) hours. Speed \(= 11 \div (26/60) \approx 25.4\) mph.

questions.prompt: The equation \(d = 55t\) gives the distance \(d\) (in miles) traveled in \(t\) hours. How many times the distance traveled after \(3k\) hours is the distance traveled after \(k\) hours?
questions.explanation: Distance is directly proportional to time. Tripling time triples distance.

questions.prompt: A park has an area of 11,863,808 square yards. What is the area, in square miles? (1 mile = 1,760 yards)
An object has a mass of 168 grams and a volume of 24 cubic centimeters. What is the density, in grams per cubic centimeter?
questions.explanation: Density equals mass divided by volume: \(168 \div 24 = 7\) grams per cubic centimeter.

questions.prompt: Based on the table, what is closest to the difference, in inches, between the diameters of a white birch and a pin oak after 10 years?
questions.explanation: Using the given growth rates, the estimated diameter difference after 10 years is closest to 1.3 inches.

questions.prompt: For values \(j\) and \(k\), the ratio \(j:k\) is \(11:12\). If \(j\) is multiplied by 17, what must \(k\) be multiplied by to maintain the same ratio?
questions.explanation: To keep a ratio the same, both quantities must be multiplied by the same factor. Therefore, \(k\) must also be multiplied by 17.
